fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: District of Columbia Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to District of Columbia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity district of columbia, how to transfer pharmacist license to district of columbia, pharmacist compact district of columbia, district of columbia pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: District of Columbia Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: DC Department of Health, Health Regulation and Licensing Administration (HRLA)
board.mailing_address: DC Health, Health Regulation & Licensing Administration, 899 North Capitol Street NE, Washington, DC 20002

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in District of Columbia.

steps.title: Create an Account
steps.description: Register as a new user on the DC Health Licensing Portal (https://app.hpla.doh.dc.gov/Applicant/Default.aspx) by clicking 'New User' and following the prompts to set up your profile.

steps.title: Initiate Application
steps.description: Log in to your newly created account, select 'New Application' from the dashboard, and choose 'Pharmacist' from the profession list. Then, specify your application type (e.g., 'Pharmacist by Examination' or 'Pharmacist by Endorsement').

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the electronic application form within the DC Health Licensing Portal, providing your personal, educational, and professional history, including any previous licenses.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload all necessary documents directly to the DC Health Licensing Portal. This may include official transcripts, proof of intern hours, NABP e-Profile ID, NAPLEX/MPJE scores (if applicable), and verification of licensure from all states where you have ever held a license.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee of $230 through the DC Health Licensing Portal using a credit/debit card.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow instructions provided in the DC Health Licensing Portal or by the Board of Pharmacy to complete the required fingerprint-based criminal background check (CBC).

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a District of Columbia pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does District of Columbia have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in District of Columbia. To work in District of Columbia, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to District of Columbia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in District of Columbia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in District of Columbia
hero.dek: To become a licensed Pharmacist in the District of Columbia, applicants must apply through the DC Health Licensing Portal, completing all required sections and submitting supporting documentation.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the DC Health Licensing Portal, click 'New User' to create an account by providing personal details, security questions, and creating a username and password.
